nodes.name: 理查德·鮑德勒·夏普
nodes.summary: 理查德·鮑德勒·夏普是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联33种鸟类，包括白眉田雞、Bates's nightjar、Bates's swift等33种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 约翰·古尔德
nodes.summary: 约翰·古尔德是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联71种鸟类，包括绿棉凫、凍原豆雁、黄腹角雉等71种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 雨林
nodes.summary: 雨林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联99种鸟类，包括叢塚雉、鳳冠孔雀雉、Amboyna cuckoo-dove等99种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 人类活动扰动
nodes.summary: 旅游、施工和高频活动影响繁殖或觅食。

nodes.name: 可岛雀
nodes.summary: 可岛雀（Cocos finch，学名 Pinaroloxias inornata），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联4个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括人类活动扰动。

nodes.name: 加拉帕戈斯群岛
nodes.summary: 加拉帕戈斯群岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联16种鸟类，包括白臉針尾鴨、Pied-billed grebe、Dark-billed cuckoo等16种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumbThe Cocos finch (Pinaroloxias inornata) or Cocos Island finch, is the only one of Darwin's finches not native to the Galápagos Islands, and the only member of the genus Pinaroloxias

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumbThe Cocos finch (Pinaroloxias inornata) or Cocos Island finch, is the only one of Darwin's finches not native to the Galápagos Islands, and the only member of the genus Pinaroloxias

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|311x311px|Cocos Island viewed from a helicopter The Cocos finch is exclusively found in Cocos Island (which takes up a 1,997 km2 area), 580 km southeast from Costa Rica. Cocos finches are not known to have traveled outside of Cocos Island, making its habitat and reproduction range to only be of the entire island. The habitat of the Cocos finch is mostly within hibiscus thickets, closed-canopy forest, and woodland forest. The climate within Cocos Island is described to be a typical rainforest climate with high humidity and chances for rainfall

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: While there has been increasing tourism around the island, there is no evidence of high disturbance rates from these tourists to affect the wildlife in the island including the Cocos finch. Despite having a small distribution, due to the predation and disturbance rate of Cocos finches being low, along with a steady population rate, the IUCN deems the Cocos finch to be Least Concern. Currently, there are estimated to be about 6,000–15,000 Cocos finches on Cocos Island
